additionalTime: 10 minutes tomato and onion seasoning time plus 5 minutes sandwich pressing
author: Robert Carr
description: A cold Italian grinder sandwich with thin-sliced ham, Genoa salami, capicola, provolone, garlic-oregano mayo, seasoned tomatoes and onions, chopped giardiniera, pepperoncini, and a red wine vinaigrette grinder salad.

groceryIngredients.item: Italian sub roll or sturdy hoagie roll

groceryIngredients.display: paper-thin hot capicola or deli ham
iceberg lettuce or shredded iceberg lettuce

ingredients: 1 Italian sub roll or sturdy hoagie roll, 6 to 7 inches long, split but not fully separated
1 1/2 ounces hot capicola or additional deli ham, sliced paper-thin
1 small ripe tomato, sliced 1/4 inch thick
A few very thin slices red onion
1 (12-ounce) jar sliced pepperoncini peppers, using 2 tablespoons peppers and 2 teaspoons pepperoncini brine
1 (12-ounce) jar mild or hot giardiniera, using 2 tablespoons drained and finely chopped vegetables
1 small garlic clove, finely grated or minced to a paste
1/4 ounce Parmesan cheese, finely grated, about 2 tablespoons loosely packed, divided
1/8 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt or a small pinch Morton kosher salt, divided
1/4 teaspoon black pepper, freshly ground if possible, divided
instructions: Place the sliced tomato and red onion in a shallow bowl or on a plate.
Drizzle the tomato and onion with 1 teaspoon extra-virgin olive oil.
Season the tomato and onion with a small pinch of Diamond Crystal kosher salt and a pinch of black pepper.
Turn the tomatoes and onion gently with your hands or tongs until the oil, salt, and pepper coat both sides of the tomato slices.
Let the seasoned tomatoes and onion sit for 10 minutes while making the mayo and grinder salad.

Make the garlic-oregano mayo in a small bowl by stirring together 3 tablespoons mayonnaise, 1 finely grated small garlic clove, 1 teaspoon Dijon mustard, 1 teaspoon red wine vinegar, 1 teaspoon pepperoncini brine, 1 tablespoon finely grated Parmesan cheese, 1/4 teaspoon dried oregano, a pinch of garlic powder, a tiny pinch of Diamond Crystal kosher salt, and a pinch of black pepper.
Taste the garlic-oregano mayo; it should be tangy, salty, garlicky, and slightly sharp from the vinegar and pepperoncini brine.
Refrigerate the garlic-oregano mayo while preparing the salad.
Drain 2 tablespoons sliced pepperoncini peppers and 2 tablespoons giardiniera well so the finished sandwich stays crisp instead of watery.
Finely chop the 2 tablespoons drained giardiniera into pieces about 1/4 inch wide.

Make the grinder salad dressing in a medium bowl by whisking together the remaining 1 teaspoon red wine vinegar, remaining 1 teaspoon extra-virgin olive oil, remaining 1 teaspoon pepperoncini brine, remaining 1/4 teaspoon dried oregano, remaining garlic powder, 1/8 teaspoon onion powder, a pinch of red pepper flakes if using, a tiny pinch of Diamond Crystal kosher salt, and the remaining black pepper.
Add the 1 cup finely shredded iceberg lettuce, 2 tablespoons drained sliced pepperoncini peppers, and 2 tablespoons finely chopped giardiniera to the bowl with the dressing.
Toss the grinder salad for 30 to 45 seconds, until every strand of lettuce is lightly coated and the pepperoncini and giardiniera are evenly distributed.
Open the split Italian sub roll or hoagie roll without separating the hinge.
If the roll is very thick, pinch out a shallow strip of bread from the inside of the top roll so the sandwich closes around the filling.
Spread about 1 tablespoon garlic-oregano mayo on the inside bottom half of the roll.
Spread about 1 tablespoon garlic-oregano mayo on the inside top half of the roll.
Place 1 slice provolone cheese on the bottom half of the mayo-coated roll.
Press 1 slice provolone cheese onto the inside top half of the mayo-coated roll so both sides of the bread have a cheese barrier.
Fold and layer 2 ounces paper-thin smoked deli ham over the bottom provolone.
Fold and layer 1 1/2 ounces paper-thin Genoa salami over the ham.
Fold and layer 1 1/2 ounces paper-thin hot capicola or additional deli ham over the salami.
Lift the seasoned tomato slices and red onion from their bowl, leaving extra tomato juices behind.
Place the seasoned tomato and red onion on the sandwich.
Sprinkle the remaining finely grated Parmesan cheese over the tomatoes and onion.
Pile the dressed grinder salad onto the sandwich.
Close the sandwich firmly so the dressed lettuce compresses into the meats and cheese.
Wrap the closed sandwich tightly in parchment paper or aluminum foil.
Press the wrapped sandwich under a cutting board, sheet pan, or heavy skillet for 5 minutes so the roll absorbs the mayo and vinaigrette without falling apart.
Cut the cold Italian grinder sandwich in half through the wrapper, then unwrap and serve immediately.
notes: This is a no-cook sandwich, so the flavor comes from strong seasoning: garlic-oregano mayo, red wine vinegar, pepperoncini brine, chopped giardiniera, salty provolone, and seasoned tomatoes.
Ask the deli counter for the ham, salami, and capicola sliced paper-thin. Very thin slices fold into layers and are easier to bite through.
Use 1 garlic clove in the mayo if the cloves are very large, very fresh, or sharply spicy. Use 2 small to medium cloves when you want a stronger garlic bite.
Use hot capicola and hot giardiniera for a spicier sandwich, or use extra deli ham and mild giardiniera for a milder version.
Do not skip the 10-minute tomato and onion seasoning step. Cold raw tomatoes and onions need salt, pepper, and olive oil to taste finished inside a sandwich.
Drain the pepperoncini and giardiniera before adding them to the lettuce. Too much jar liquid makes the rolls soggy before the first bite.
Dress the iceberg lettuce right before assembly so it stays crisp but still picks up the oregano vinaigrette.
The recipe uses a restrained amount of salt because the deli meats, provolone, Parmesan, pepperoncini, and giardiniera already bring plenty of salt.
Use sturdy Italian sub rolls or hoagie rolls with a hinge. Very soft sandwich bread can tear once the grinder salad and seasoned tomatoes go in.
Splitting the provolone between the bottom and top of each roll helps protect the bread from the seasoned tomatoes, onion, and dressed grinder salad.
The 5-minute wrapped press helps the sandwich hold together and lets the mayo, vinaigrette, meat, cheese, tomato, onion, and salad combine.

This sandwich is best right after pressing. For make-ahead, keep the garlic-oregano mayo, seasoned tomatoes and onions, and undressed lettuce mixture separate, then dress and assemble within 15 minutes of serving.
title: Cold Italian Grinder Sandwich
